differential_diagnoses: PASH Syndrome (Pyoderma Gangrenosum, Acne, and Hidradenitis Suppurativa)
next_steps: Skin biopsy of the advancing ulcer edge for histopathology, tissue culture (bacterial, fungal, mycobacterial), and immunofluorescence
Initiation of systemic high-dose glucocorticoid therapy
Evaluation for inflammatory bowel disease (fecal calprotectin, ileocolonoscopy)
Complete laboratory workup including serum inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP, Ferritin), ANCA, ANA, and antiphospholipid antibodies
Consideration of adjunctive therapy with biologics (e.g., TNF-alpha inhibitors or IL-1 receptor antagonists) pending response to steroids
